Introduction.p1: Hello everyone, thank you for visiting my website. My name is <bold>Do Quyet Thang</bold>.
Introduction.p2: I graduated from FPT University in 2024. At the time I am writing this, it is 2026, and I am currently working as a Tester. My daily work mainly includes checking tickets on Jira, writing test cases, and performing manual testing, mostly regression testing.
Introduction.p3: However, after working for a while, I realized that my current job does not offer many long-term growth opportunities. The role mainly focuses on internal company work, so I have not had many chances to learn more deeply about modern testing tools and techniques. That made me feel that I need to find a clearer direction to develop myself further in the IT field.
Introduction.p4: Back when I was still a student, I had already realized that I was not a good fit for my major, which made me lose motivation and direction for a while. After graduation, I tried several jobs related to IT, but they still did not feel truly suitable. In the end, I decided to move into Testing because I believe this field offers many growth opportunities and matches my strengths better.
Introduction.li1: My IT foundation is still weak.
Introduction.li2: I am not good at coding, and my basic knowledge is still limited.
Introduction.li3: If I want to grow further as a tester, I need to relearn and strengthen my knowledge seriously.
Introduction.p5: At the moment, I am focusing on rebuilding my fundamentals and improving the skills I need to pursue Testing in a more serious and sustainable way.
Introduction.p6: This website is where I record my learning journey, work experience, and what I have accumulated while following the Testing path.
Introduction.p7: I see this as a small milestone to track my own progress and prepare myself better for the road ahead.
Introduction.p8: Thank you for taking the time to visit and read my story.

goals.title: Career Goals
goals.longTerm: Automation Tester, Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET)

items.Jira: Jira: creating tickets, tracking progress, and managing work effectively
items.SQL: SQL: basic database querying and data handling (Join, Group By, Having, Subquery)
items.ISTQB: Process knowledge: understanding the Software Testing Life Cycle (STLC) and Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC)

tasks: Checked tickets on Jira, tracked progress, and coordinated work with the team.
Wrote test cases and performed manual testing, mainly regression testing.
Tested payment-related features, business workflows, and system changes.
Verified UI and user experience across related screens and functions.
Reported bugs, tracked issues, and re-tested after fixes were delivered by developers.
Joined a training program focused on BrSE orientation and working skills in the IT environment.
Improved Korean for professional use and workplace communication skills.
Learned about the software development process and collaboration between teams in a project.
Became familiar with business documents, technical documents, and requirement communication.
Supported testing-related tasks and data processing work based on instructions.
Learned the basics of the software testing process and bug reporting.
Improved attention to detail, observation skills, and task execution based on project requirements.
Worked with the team to complete assigned tasks on time.

PracticesPage.title: Practices
PracticesPage.empty: I haven't done any practice yet. Please check back later.

0.title: Become a Tester in 6 Months
0.description: A complete self-study roadmap for transitioning into a QA/Tester role from scratch — covering fundamentals, manual testing, API testing, automation, and job preparation.

1.title: HTML Fundamentals
1.description: Learn the building blocks of the web — document structure, semantic elements, forms, and accessibility basics.

2.title: CSS Fundamentals
2.description: Learn how to style web pages using selectors, the box model, flexbox, grid, and responsive design principles.

3.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
3.description: Learn the core concepts of JavaScript — variables, functions, DOM manipulation, events, and asynchronous programming.

TesterPlanPage.title: Become a Tester in 6 Months
TesterPlanPage.overview: A self-study roadmap for transitioning into a QA/Tester role from scratch. No strong IT background and no budget for paid courses — just steady effort in the evenings and on weekends.

phase1.title: Software Testing Fundamentals
phase1.goal: Understand what testing is, its role in software development, and essential QA concepts.

phase2.title: Manual Testing & Test Case Design
phase2.goal: Learn how to write effective test cases and test plans, and apply black-box and white-box testing techniques.

phase3.title: API Testing with Postman
phase3.goal: Practice REST API testing: sending requests, writing assertions, and organizing collections.

phase4.title: Automation Testing Basics
phase4.goal: Learn to write basic automated UI tests using popular free tools.

phase5.title: SQL & Database Testing
phase5.goal: Understand how to verify data at the database level using basic SQL queries.

phase6.title: Job Preparation
phase6.goal: Build a portfolio, prepare a CV, and get ready for junior Tester interviews.

HtmlPlanPage.title: HTML Fundamentals
HtmlPlanPage.overview: A 5-day plan to learn the basic building blocks of the web. It covers document structure, semantic HTML, forms, media, and accessibility basics.

day1.title: Document Structure & Core Tags
day1.goal: Understand the structure of an HTML document and learn the most commonly used tags.

day2.title: Semantic HTML
day2.goal: Learn to write meaningful HTML using semantic elements that improve readability and SEO.

day3.title: Forms & Input Types
day3.goal: Build interactive forms using different input types and understand the basics of form submission.

day4.title: Tables, Media & Embedding
day4.goal: Display structured data with tables and embed media such as images, videos, and audio.

day5.title: Accessibility & Best Practices
day5.goal: Write accessible and clean HTML that works well for all users and assistive technologies.

CssPlanPage.title: CSS Fundamentals
CssPlanPage.overview: A 4-day plan to learn how to style web pages. It covers the box model, flexbox, grid, responsive design, and basic animations.

day1.title: Selectors, Specificity & Box Model
day1.goal: Understand how CSS rules are applied and how the box model controls layout and spacing.

day2.title: Flexbox
day2.goal: Use flexbox to build one-dimensional layouts with flexible and responsive behavior.

day3.title: Grid & Responsive Design
day3.goal: Create two-dimensional layouts with CSS Grid and make pages adapt to different screen sizes.

day4.title: Transitions, Animations & CSS Variables
day4.goal: Add motion and dynamic styling using CSS transitions, keyframe animations, and custom properties.

JsPlanPage.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
JsPlanPage.overview: A 1-week plan to learn the core concepts of JavaScript. It covers variables, functions, DOM manipulation, events, and asynchronous programming.

day1.title: Variables, Data Types & Operators
day1.goal: Understand the basic building blocks of JavaScript: how to store and work with data.

day2.title: Control Flow & Functions
day2.goal: Control program logic with conditionals and loops, and organize code into reusable functions.

day3.title: Arrays & Objects
day3.goal: Work with collections of data using arrays and objects, including common built-in methods.

day4.title: DOM Manipulation
day4.goal: Read and modify HTML elements and styles directly using JavaScript.

day5.title: Events
day5.goal: Respond to user interactions such as clicks, inputs, and keyboard actions.

day6.title: Asynchronous Programming
day6.goal: Understand asynchronous JavaScript and how to fetch data from APIs.

day7.title: ES6+ Features & Practice
day7.goal: Review modern JavaScript syntax and apply everything learned in a small hands-on project.
